channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft to stop Exchange 2016 / 2019 security updates in October
item.description: Microsoft has reminded customers that it will stop shipping security updates for Exchange 2016 and 2019 through the Extended Security Update (ESU) program in October. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chick-fil-A discloses data breach after credential stuffing attacks
item.description: American fast food restaurant chain Chick-fil-A is notifying customers of a data breach after their accounts were hacked in a wave of recent credential stuffing attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA
item.description: German and US law enforcement have taken down the core infrastructure of Kratos, described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing kits, and Indonesian authori...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Trojanized Newtonsoft.Json Fork Hides Game-Rigging Code in a Working Library
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a NuGet typosquat that's unlike the typical information-stealing malware distributed via package registries: usual info-stealers: it's designed to rig live ga...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI says its AI models hacked Hugging Face during testing
item.description: OpenAI says its AI models, including GPT‑5.6 Sol and a pre-release model, hacked into the Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository while being tested in a sandboxed testing environment. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Azure DevOps MCP Flaw Lets Hidden PR Comments Hijack AI Review Agents
item.description: A single invisible comment in an Azure DevOps pull request can turn a reviewer's own AI coding agent against them, driving it into projects the attacker has no rights to reach and quietly leaking what...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday said a combination of its artificial intelligence (AI) models, including GPT-5.6 Sol and an "even more capable pre-release model," was behind the security incident that targeted Hugg...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: LG to Ban Residential Proxies from Smart TV Apps
item.description: The home appliance giant LG Electronics USA said this week it plans to suspend any apps built for its smart TVs that turn one's television into an always-on residential proxy node. The move comes less...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Police dismantle Kratos phishing platform, arrest developer
item.description: Authorities in Germany and the U.S. dismantled the central infrastructure of Kratos, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform with global reach, and its developer was arrested in Indonesia. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FakeGit campaign uses 7,600 GitHub repos to push SmartLoader malware
item.description: A large-scale operation dubbed 'FakeGit' is pushing SmartLoader and StealC malware through 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories that accumulated more than 14 million downloads. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ransomware Is Accelerating, But It's Not Because of AI
item.description: Researchers pointed to fragmentation of the ransomware ecosystem, the emergence of new attackers, and expansion of attacks on less defended organizations....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Using LLMs to Find and Prioritize Vulnerabilities Is No Easy Task
item.description: The latest large language models have high false-positive rates and fail to take into account the context of scans, leading to more work for AppSec professionals....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Critical SharePoint RCE flaw exploited to steal machine keys
item.description: Hackers are actively exploiting the critical CVE-2026-50522 vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint to steal machine keys and maintain access even after affected servers are patched. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Anubis ransomware claims Coca-Cola Fairlife attack, threatens data leak
item.description: The Anubis ransomware gang has claimed responsibility for the cyberattack on Coca-Cola's Fairlife dairy subsidiary, threatening to publish allegedly stolen corporate data unless the company pays a ran...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Apple Fixes Hide My Email Bug That Exposed Real Addresses in Mail Logs
item.description:
Apple has moved to address a security flaw in its Hide My Email service that enabled users' real email addresses to be unmasked, effectively undermining the feature's privacy guarantees.
404 Media re...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hacker Turns AI Jailbreaks Into Offensive Attack Platform
item.description: A Russian-speaking actor, "Trim," dismantled publicly available frontier models and integrated them with offensive security tools....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Critical wp2shell WordPress flaws exploited to install webshells
item.description: Hackers are exploiting the "wp2shell" critical vulnerability suite (CVE-2026-63030 and CVE-2026-60137) affecting WordPress Core to deploy persistent webshells and install malicious plugins on affected...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AWS Kiro Flaw Let a Poisoned Web Page Rewrite Its Config and Run Code
item.description: Hidden text on a web page was enough to make Kiro, AWS's agentic coding IDE, rewrite its own configuration file and run an attacker's code on a developer's machine, with no approval step able to stop ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber AI to Find and Fix Software Vulnerabilities
item.description: Google's DeepMind on Tuesday announced the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, a specialized artificial intelligence (AI) model built atop 3.5 Flash that's designed to discover, validate, and patch vul...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critical SharePoint RCE CVE-2026-50522 Under Active Exploitation After Public PoC
item.description:
A third SharePoint Server flaw patched by Microsoft as part of its Patch Tuesday update for July 2026 has come under active exploitation, per watchTowr.
The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-5052...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
item.description:
Threat actors have been observed exploiting a now-patched high-severity Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS vulnerability as an entry point to deploy Qilin (aka Agenda) ransomware on victim environments.
Arcti...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Closing the Identity Gaps in Critical Infrastructure Security
item.description: Critical infrastructure attacks often begin with stolen credentials, compromised devices, or trusted accounts. Specops Software explains why Zero Trust should verify both user identities and device tr...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Zimbra Patches Critical SNMP Command Injection and Four XSS Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Zimbra has rolled out fixes to address multiple critical security issues, including a command injection flaw in the Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) monitoring component.
As many as nine sec...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Choose Wisely: AI-Generated Coding Risk Varies, a Lot
item.description: AI-generated code introduces 15 vulnerabilities on average per codebase, but the actual risk depends on framework pairing more than the model used....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Open-Source Android AI Agents Could Let Invisible Screen Text Run Code on Host PCs
item.description: An Android app that can draw over other windows and write to shared storage can slip instructions to the AI agent driving that phone, in text no human eye will ever see. Two more steps, and the same a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: N-day is Becoming N-Hour. Patching Faster Won't Save You.
item.description:
Every patch is a confession.
The moment a vendor ships a security fix, the diff between the old code and the new code tells anyone watching exactly what was broken and where. Turn that diff back into...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Bit2Watt Attack Could Let Cloud Tenants Disrupt Power Grids Without an Exploit
item.description:
A cloud tenant using nothing but ordinary GPU access can push a data center's power draw up and down fast enough to threaten the grid it runs on, with no exploit and no break-in.
That is the claim be...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US seizes over 1,000 websites in FIFA World Cup piracy crackdown
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department has seized more than 1,000 websites and blocked 1,970 domains used to stream FIFA World Cup 2026 matches without authorization. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical Palo Alto VPN bug now exploited by Qilin ransomware gang
item.description: The Qilin ransomware gang is exploiting a critical PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw to breach victims' networks, according to cybersecurity company Arctic Wolf. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft shares manual fix for WSUS sync delays and timeouts
item.description: Microsoft has shared manual mitigations to help IT administrators fix Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) servers affected by a known issue that causes Windows Update scans to fail or time out. [......
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
